Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

Name: Beedrill ex
Effects: This attack does 110 damage for each of your Beedrill and Beedrill ex in play.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no Retreat Cost, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Chespin
Flavor-Text: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Name: Quilladin
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [G] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won’t start fights.

Name: Chesnaught
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: Put up to 3 Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind it when it flies.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It keeps a twig in its tail. Using friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

Name: Delphox
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to all Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

Name: Mega Pyroar ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Remoraid
Flavor-Text: The water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.

Name: Octillery
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its instinct is to bury itself in holes. It often steals the nesting holes of others to sleep in them.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Each player may attach up to 3 Basic Energy cards from their hand to their Pokémon in any way they like. Your opponent does this first.
Flavor-Text: It carries food rolled up in its tail. It has a habit of sharing food with people lost in the mountains.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Keldeo has strengthened its resolve for battle, filling its body with power and changing its form.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Mega Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may discard a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Place 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may put a [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand and have this attack do 80 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Bergmite
Flavor-Text: It blocks opponents’ attacks with the ice that shields its body. If the ice breaks, this Pokémon uses cold air to quickly create new ice.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 60 damage for each Basic [W] Energy card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Its ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way.

Name: Wimpod
Flavor-Text: It’s nature’s cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

Name: Golisopod
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

Name: Mareep
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy coat doubles in size when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. Flaaffy can fire hair that zaps on impact.

Name: Ampharos
Ability-Effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, attacks used by this Pokémon do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flashing Bolt.
Flavor-Text: The tail’s tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its patagial membranes and released while it is gliding.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Mega Floette ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Search your deck for up to 4 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Espurr
Effects: If you played Emma from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It keeps its intense psychic power from leaking out by using its ears to cover the organs emitting that power.

Name: Meowstic
Effects: You may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

Name: Phantump
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Choose a card in your hand that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. If you do, place 2 damage counters on the Pokémon you evolved in this way. You can’t use this Ability during your first turn.
Flavor-Text: According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Energy can’t be attached from your opponent’s hand to the Defending Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Flavor-Text: It was recently discovered that the different varieties of Pumpkaboo vary not only in size but also in the size of the souls they collect.

Name: Gourgeist ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Transformation Tome cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: It is far stronger than it appears. If a Phanpy is swinging its trunk around and your arm gets hit by it, your arm bone will shatter.

Name: Donphan
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Donphan is normally a calm Pokémon, but once it is enraged, it will curl its body into a ball and charge at you while rolling.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

Name: Claydol
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

Name: Mega Gallade ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

Name: Golbat
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

Name: Qwilfish
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Experienced fishers say they try to catch Qwilfish in the brief moment that these Pokémon become defenseless just after launching poisonous spikes.

Name: Stunky
Flavor-Text: The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a quarter away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by spraying a repugnant fluid from its tail, but the stench dulls after a few squirts.

Name: Krookodile ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 140 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool attached do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text: Camouflaged as rotten kelp, this Pokémon sprays liquid poison to take down prey that approach unawares.

Name: Beldum
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

Name: Metang
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won’t be scratched if it collides with a jet.

Name: Metagross
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
You may discard 3 [M] Energy from this Pokémon and have this attack do 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn’t very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

Name: Ferrothorn
Ability-Effect: During your opponent’s turn, if this Pokémon is discarded from your deck by an effect of an attack or Ability from your opponent’s Pokémon, or by an effect of your opponent’s Item or Supporter cards, discard the top 8 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

Name: Cobalion ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Move any amount of [M] Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Dragalge ex
Effects: Discard all Pokémon Tools and Special Energy from all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, place 16 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Goomy
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to the slimy membrane covering its body, punches or kicks from its enemies slide right off it.

Name: Sliggoo
Flavor-Text: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: When your opponent’s Active Pokémon retreats, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, Energy for its Retreat Cost is not discarded, and they don’t switch Pokémon. The effect of Slimy Sliding doesn’t stack.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This very friendly dragon Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play that has “Tauros” in its name. This attack does 50 damage to the chosen Pokémon for each heads. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They fight each other by locking horns. The herd’s protectors take pride in their battle-scarred horns.

Name: Patrat
Ability-Effect: Damage counters on each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) can’t be moved to other Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If any of them are heads, your opponent reveals their hand. For each heads, choose a card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Using luminescent matter within its body, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

Name: Minccino
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon like things neat and tidy. They are always sweeping and dusting their habitat, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino ex
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Adversity Policy
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has Weakness to your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s type, is in the Active Spot, and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 3 cards.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Ange Floette
Description:
You can put this card into play only if you discard a Prism Tower in play, and you can put this card into play during the same turn you play Prism Tower.
Each Mega Floette ex in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets +150 HP.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: AZ’s Tranquility
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you moved a Pokémon ex to your Bench in this way, heal 80 damage from that Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Emma
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Pokémon you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Great Haul Net
Description:
Choose 1 or both:
• Shuffle up to 3 [W] Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle up to 3 Basic [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Philippe
Description: Attach up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your [M] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Prism Tower
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard 2 cards from their hand in order to draw a card.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Roxie’s Performance
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, their Poisoned Pokémon can’t retreat. (This includes newly Poisoned Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Special Red Card
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining.Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Transformation Tome
Description: You must play 2 Transformation Tome cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)Choose a Basic Pokémon in your discard pile and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Bubbly [W] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [W] Energy.The [W] Pokémon this card is attached to recovers from all Special Conditions and can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.

Name: Magnetic [M] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [M] Energy.The [M] Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.

Name: Nitro [R] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [R] Energy.If this card is discarded by an effect of an attack used by the [R] Pokémon this card is attached to, put this card into your hand after attack damage and effects.

Name: Chespin
Flavor-Text: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Effects: You may put a [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand and have this attack do 80 more damage.

Search your deck for up to 4 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Pokémon you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Jumbo Ice Cream
Description: Heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon that has 3 or more Energy attached.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Philippe
Description: Attach up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your [M] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Prism Tower
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard 2 cards from their hand in order to draw a card.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Description: During your opponent’s next turn, their Poisoned Pokémon can’t retreat. (This includes newly Poisoned Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Special Red Card
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining.Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Surfing Beach
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may switch their Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of their Benched [W] Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Tool Scrapper
Description: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s) and discard them.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Mega Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may discard a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Place 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may put a [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand and have this attack do 80 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Floette ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Search your deck for up to 4 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Dragalge ex
Effects: Discard all Pokémon Tools and Special Energy from all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, place 16 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Cinccino ex
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: AZ’s Tranquility
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you moved a Pokémon ex to your Bench in this way, heal 80 damage from that Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Roxie’s Performance
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, their Poisoned Pokémon can’t retreat. (This includes newly Poisoned Pokémon.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mega Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may discard a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Place 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may put a [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand and have this attack do 80 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
